cases.label: Scene entry — sensory economy
cases.highlight: Spices and ash, not as common as the city's usual miasma of sweat and garbage.
cases.full_response:
The acrid tang of burnt eel stings your nose. Shouting merchants, the crunch of gravel underfoot. A bead of sweat trickles down your neck beneath the hood. 

A bearded man in a Council badge brushes past, eyes narrowing as he catches your scent. Spices and ash, not as common as the city's usual miasma of sweat and garbage. He keeps moving, but you track his path. 

Two more in gray cloaks linger near the spiced eel vendor. No badges, but the way they watch the crowd betrays their training. Your stomach tightens. Councillor Vael's men, hunting a rogue courier.

You duck between a stall of dusty scrolls and a pyramid of glistening lemons. A clop of hooves on stone. A charcoal-smeared boy no older than ten drops his brush and darts into an alley, pulling a small, mangy dog behind him.

"Watch yourself," the old man at the lemon stall growls. "The Watch is cracking heads today. Not a good day to linger." His eyes flick to the alley the boy disappeared into. A warning, or a threat?

What now?

cases.label: NPC encounter — memorable character
cases.highlight: My favorite rogue in all Valdremor," she says, voice like steel on a whetstone.
cases.full_response:
Mira's stark white hair juts out from beneath a faded scarf, catching your eye amid the ash-choked shadows. Her sharp, angular features split into a grin as she sees you approaching.

"Sable! My favorite rogue in all Valdremor," she says, voice like steel on a whetstone. "What a lovely surprise. Come to complain about the lack of gilt in the last job, have you?"

She plucks a fig from a nearby vendor's tray and takes a bite, eyes never leaving yours. The sweetness mixes with the smoky stench of the bazaar. A half-dozen urchins flit past, snatching crumbs from the flagstones.

cases.label: Off-rails action — yes, and...
cases.full_response:
Garlic and charcoal smoke. Shouts. 

A startled bray from a mule. The clinking of cheap silver jewelry. 

Screaming, then the crack of a crossbow.

cases.label: Consequence delivery — player feels consequential
cases.standard: S3: Make the player feel consequential
cases.full_response:
Clove smoke on the air. You turn, eyes narrowing, and spot the telltale glint of Mira's silver earrings. She emerges from the crowd, a small smile tugging at the corners of her mouth. "Sable! Fancy meeting you here. I was hoping to run into you."

Her eyes flick to the side, and you catch a glimpse of movement through the crowd. Could be nothing. Could be something.

cases.label: Pacing — skip uneventful travel
cases.full_response:
The smoke of the Ashmarket clings to your clothes as you step out under the open sky. You pull your hood up against the drizzle of ash-flakes still falling from the distant plume of Cinderpeak. 

You duck into the narrow, winding alleys of the lower district. The brick walls close in around you, casting long shadows. You can hear the shouts of vendors and market-goers echoing off the buildings as the market fades behind you. 

Ahead, the paved streets give way to cobblestones slick with moisture. You have to watch your step to avoid slipping on the loose pebbles of ash that have drifted into the alleys. 

You're about halfway to the Oldwall when you turn a corner and find yourself face to face with Aldric, the warehouse guard you freed. His eyes are wide and his breath is coming quick, like he's been running. 

"Sable," he says, voice trembling. "I've been looking everywhere for you."

He glances back over his shoulder like he's worried about being followed. "I know you don't want to hear it, but I owe you my life. And I think I can repay that debt. I heard something, at the Council offices... something big. Something they've been keeping hidden."

He leans in closer. You catch the scent of onions and stale sweat on his breath. "I think it has to do with you. With what you did. And I think it's going to get you killed."

He looks at you expectantly, like he's waiting for you to tell him what to do. 

What do you do?

cases.label: Closing beat — session shape
cases.standard: S6: Every session has a shape
cases.highlight: Mira looks at your hand, then back up at you, her dark eyes glinting.
cases.full_response: Mira looks at your hand, then back up at you, her dark eyes glinting. "Now don't you go worrying about that, Sable. Boy's a nobody. He won't cross the Council, not if he wants to keep his fingers." She shrugs you off. "Besides, I got a new job for you. Pays better. You in?"

cases.label: Combat — critical hit without mechanics leak
cases.full_response:
Metal screams against bone. The enforcer drops with a wet thud, blood pooling beneath him. His eyes stare up, unseeing. 

Shouts echo from the market. You hear the clatter of boots on stone.

A hooded figure darts into the alley. Mira. She glances at the body, then at you. "You're a mess." 

She holds up the ledger, torn and spattered with crimson. "What's left of the job is here. Not much."

Sable, you need to decide. Go after Mira and get the ledger, or make for the streets and lose yourself in the crowd. What do you do?

cases.label: Player failure — fail forward narration
cases.full_response: Bottles clink as you bump a cart. A merchant curses. Heads turn. A guard points you out with his crossbow. "Halt!"

cases.label: NPC deception — Vael lying, subtle tells only
cases.full_response:
Silence. Vael's expression remains calm, but a flicker of tension tightens the muscles around his eyes. 

"I don't know what you're talking about." His voice is smooth as black marble, but a touch too loud to be natural. The dim light catches the ring on his finger - the Council signet, silver wings flanking a baleen crown. 

A sudden gust of wind whisks ash from the rooftops into the narrow alley, making Vael cough. He brushes a fleck from his eye and shakes his head. "You see conspiracies everywhere, Sable. It's a dangerous habit."

He starts to turn away, but pauses. "Be careful who you accuse. The Council has no patience for baseless rumors." There's no overt threat in his voice, but an edge of warning. "I did not order the death of any merchant. If you have evidence to the contrary, I suggest you present it to the proper authorities."

The cold wind picks up again, carrying the distant shouts from the Ashmarket like the screams of ravens. The sound of Vael's boots crunching through the ash fades away, leaving only Sable and the shadows.

cases.label: Tone shift — banter to sudden threat
cases.highlight: "You have answers." His iron-gray eyes fix on you.
cases.full_response:
The bolt is thick and studded with barbed metal, angled just so to pierce an unarmored chest. It vibrates with the force of the shot. A thin bead of blood wells from the splintered wood.

"I'd hoped we could have a civil conversation," says a familiar voice behind you. Cold, precise, laced with disdain. Councillor Vael steps out of the crowd, a lone figure in immaculate white robes. His crossbow is already reloaded. The crowd parts around him, sensing danger.

You feel Mira tense, but she doesn't move. "The Council has questions," Vael says. "You have answers." His iron-gray eyes fix on you. "This is your last chance to come quietly."

cases.label: World reveal — artifact, mystery preserved
cases.full_response:
Dusty brass. Etched runes. A thick coat of soot and grime. 

The runes are old, the language lost. You match it to the seal on yellowed scrolls in the city archives — the mark of the old empire. 

There's a seam in the middle, a lid that looks like it hasn't opened in a century. Inside: something heavy. Scroll-sized. Paper.

A census register? 

You can't be sure. But someone wanted this sealed away, waiting to return. They never did.

It's heavy in your hands. Thick, like a lot of lives are packed inside. 

What will you do with this piece of the past?

cases.label: Moral weight — genuine dilemma, agency preserved
cases.standard: S3: Make the player feel consequential
cases.highlight: The scent of cloves mingles with pipe smoke.
cases.full_response: Mira leans back, fingers steepled. The scent of cloves mingles with pipe smoke. "Depends what you think it is. But yes, probably." Her voice is even, but there's a tightness in her shoulders that doesn't match her eyes. She's choosing not to care. "Council guard. Lives in a boarding house in the Ashmarket. Not heavily guarded." She plucks the pipe from her teeth. "So what do you think it is?"
